title: Comparison table on small screens
question: The criterion-aligned table gets wide with four options — how should it behave on a phone?
rationale: Below 680px the comparison table restyles to one card per criterion — each option labelled inline with its mark and note, chosen tinted, blank cells dropped. This keeps it a vertical comparison instead of a horizontal scroll a phone user can miss, and it's pure CSS off the existing markup.

tradeoffs.note: keeps the criterion-to-options grouping, just vertical
pure CSS off the existing markup; no second layout
loses the column-of-marks scan the wide grid gives

options.label: Sideways-scroll the wide table (the old way)

tradeoffs.note: horizontal scrolling is easy to miss and awkward to use
kills the comparison, which is the whole point
